questions.question: What is the nature of each claimed injury for {patient_name}? (fracture, tear, strain, herniation etc.)
questions.resolved_question: What is the nature of each claimed injury for Erik M. Barrezueta-Orellana? (fracture, tear, strain, herniation etc.)
questions.answer: Erik M. Barrezueta-Orellana's claimed injuries include: lumbar spine broad-based central disc herniations at L4-L5 and L5-S1, posterior annular disc bulges at L3-L4 and L4-L5, posterior subligamentous disc bulge at L5-S1, facet hypertrophic changes at L4-L5 and L5-S1, and straightening of lordosis; cervical spine bilateral foraminal disc herniations at C5-C6, disc bulges at C1-C5 and C6-C7, strain and straightening of lordosis, broad posterior disc herniation at C6-C7; right shoulder full-thickness tear of the acromioclavicular joint capsule with joint separation, supraspinatus and infraspinatus tendinitis, labral tear, impingement syndrome, hyperemic synovitis, bursitis, sprain; right knee longitudinal tear at the anterior root attachment of the medial meniscus with joint effusion, internal derangement, thickened plica with synovitis, grade II traumatic chondromalacia of the patella lateral facet, edema, sprain; left knee focal longitudinal tear at the anterior root attachment of the lateral meniscus with joint effusion, traumatic meniscus injury, internal derangement, sprain, synovitis, edema; thoracic spine tenderness and muscle spasm; right hip internal derangement, sprain; right elbow sprain; head/brain injuries including loss of consciousness, post-concussion syndrome, memory and cognitive dysfunction, closed head injury, bilateral tinnitus, blurred vision, depression, anxiety, post-traumatic headache, dizziness, insomnia, mood changes, photophobia, phonophobia; other injuries include myofascial pain syndrome, myalgia, right hand pain and weakness, abdominal pain and swelling, vertigo, post-traumatic stress disorder, and difficulty sleeping. No fractures were reported.

questions.question: Are any of {patient_name} injuries described as pre-existing or degenerative in any document?
questions.resolved_question: Are any of Erik M. Barrezueta-Orellana injuries described as pre-existing or degenerative in any document?
questions.answer: Yes. According to the defendants' assertions in the legal documents, Erik M. Barrezueta-Orellana pursued medical treatment for pre-existing degenerative conditions while claiming trauma-related injuries from the alleged accident.
